title: buildah bud fails on RUN apt...
body:
```
buildah -v
buildah version 0.4 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```
```
root@ubuntu-xenial:/home/ubuntu/# buildah bud -f Dockerfile -t btl:latest
STEP 1: FROM debian:stable-slim
STEP 2: RUN apt-get update; apt-get install -y iputils-ping net-tools openssl libssl1.0.2
json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string
error building: error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[apt-get update; apt-get install -y iputils-ping net-tools openssl libssl1.0.2] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN apt-get update; apt-get install -y iputils-ping net-tools openssl libssl1.0.2 Original:RUN apt-get update; apt-get install -y iputils-ping net-tools openssl libssl1.0.2}: exit status 1
```

**Dockerfile**
```
FROM debian:stable-slim

RUN apt-get update; apt-get install -y iputils-ping net-tools openssl libssl1.0.2

RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

comments.body: This sounds like an incompatible version of runc (specifically, that the configuration we generate for it isn't in the format that it expects).  What do you get from running `runc --version` on the host?

Tried with the latest binary: [runc](https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/v1.0.0-rc4/runc.amd64)
```
runc -v
runc version 1.0.0-rc4
spec: 1.0.0
```
Seems ok. Thanks @nalind
